answer.root_cause: Firmware update introduced a bug affecting signal quality
diagnostic_steps: Check system logs for errors or warnings related to the antenna or radio interface around the time of the firmware update
Verify current SINR and HARQ retransmission rates against baseline or historical data to confirm the degradation

history.reasoning: Ruled out hardware issues due to no active alarms; external interference is unlikely as adjacent nodes are normal; network-wide issues are improbable given adjacent node metrics; the timing of symptoms post-firmware update suggests a software-related cause.

judge_score.feedback: The agent provided an excellent, systematic analysis. The root cause identification is strong because it correctly links the symptoms (degradation) to the most recent major event (firmware update) with explicit temporal correlation. The diagnostic steps are highly relevant, including a log check related to the suspected cause (firmware) and a proper pre/post metrics comparison. Furthermore, the reasoning explicitly rules out alternative causes (hardware and interference) using the provided data points (no alarms, normal adjacent nodes). The response is clear and well-structured.
